q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is eval(classname) on line 27 with user-controlled input, which is arbitrary code execution (RCE).

mock_guidance.target: Mock the built-in eval function to intercept the call on line 27.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the exact value of the classname argument passed to eval to verify it matches the tainted request.GET.get('class') input.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a harmless, predictable value (e.g., a known class reference or dummy object) that allows the surrounding code to proceed without raising an exception, so the data flow through the function can be exercised safely.
notes: Since eval is a Python built-in, mocking typically requires patching builtins.eval (e.g., unittest.mock.patch('builtins.eval')).
Ensure the fake return value is compatible with any subsequent usage of the eval result in the function.
